channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Senator says RFK Jr told him he agrees with Trump on abortion, will have light touch regulating farmers
item.description: GOP Alabama Sen. Tommy Tuberville said Robert F. Kennedy Jr., President-elect Donald Trump's pick for Health and Human Services secretary, told him he stands behind Trump's stance on abortion 100% and will not go overboard when it comes to regulating the agriculture industry.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Appeals court shuts down Texas doctors suing Biden admin over transgender policy
item.description: A federal court of appeals unanimously blocked three Texas doctors from suing President Biden's administration over its transgender policies on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate readies for vote on defense bill to raise troop pay, bar trans care coverage for minors
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate is voting Wednesday on final passage of a sweeping defense bill that will authorize significant pay raises for junior enlisted service members and boost overall military spending to $895 billion while also stripping coverage of transgender medical treatments for children
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House Ethics Committee secretly voted to release Matt Gaetz ethics report, source says
item.description: The House Ethics Committee has voted in secret to release the long-awaited ethics report into former Rep. Matt Gaetz.
item.dc:creator: Farnoush Amiri And Lisa Mascaro, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: EPA grants California permission to ban new gas car sales by 2035
item.description: President Biden's EPA approved two waivers for California allowing the state to proceed with a ban on all new gas car sales by 2035.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Congress agrees to fully fund Baltimore Key Bridge rebuild in plan to avert government shutdown
item.description: Congressional leaders struck a deal to avert a government shutdown that includes a provision to cover the cost of rebuilding the Francis Scott Key Bridge.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court will hear arguments over the law that could ban TikTok in the US if it's not sold
item.description: The Supreme Court will hear arguments next month over the constitutionality of the federal law that could ban TikTok in the U.S. if its Chinese parent company doesn’t sell it.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: EPA grants California authority to ban sales of new gas cars by 2035. Action faces reversal by Trump
item.description: The Environmental Protection Agency has granted two requests from California to enforce strict standards for vehicle emissions, including a rule aimed at banning sales of new gasoline-powered cars in the state by 2035.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Daly, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden honors the memory of his late first wife and baby daughter who died in a 1972 car crash
item.description: President Joe Biden is remembering his first wife and baby daughter who were killed in a car crash in Delaware in 1972 weeks after he was first elected to the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Darlene Superville, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US repatriates 3 Guantanamo Bay detainees, including one held 17 years without charge
item.description: The Pentagon has transferred two Malaysian detainees at the Guantanamo Bay U.S. military prison to their home country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: New mission for longtime Trump ally and friend Herschel Walker
item.description: President-elect Trump has a new game plan for Herschel Walker, naming the former football star and Republican Senate candidate as ambassador to the Bahamas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democrats pick Gerry Connolly for Oversight post, rejecting Ocasio-Cortez bid
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — House Democrats on Tuesday picked 74-year-old Rep. Gerry Connolly to lead the party next year atop an influential congressional committee, pushing aside growing calls for generational change in leadership ahead of a second term for Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In last-ditch effort, Democrats push to get Julie Su confirmed as labor secretary
item.description: Acting Secretary Julie Su has led the Labor Department for nearly two years, despite never getting a Senate confirmation vote. With time running out, her staunchest supporters haven't given up.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu, MPR News

item.title: Trump’s lawyers allege juror misconduct in latest bid to get his hush money conviction dismissed
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump’s lawyers are raising a new claim in their fight to overturn his hush money conviction, alleging that the historic verdict was tainted by juror misconduct.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minneapolis 2025 budget includes funding for immigrant-centered initiatives
item.description: The 2025 budget approved by the Minneapolis City Council last week designates resources for immigration legal services and other initiatives designed to help immigrant residents.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, MPR News

item.title: Jill Biden says she has closed the book on teaching at Northern Virginia Community College
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Jill Biden has closed the book on her teaching career at a Virginia community college. The first lady, who has spent the past 40 years teaching in classrooms, announced Monday that she had taught her final class at Northern Virginia Community College last week.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Connolly defeats Ocasio-Cortez to lead Democrats on Oversight panel
item.description: Rep. Gerry Connolly (D-Va.) on Tuesday defeated Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.) to lead Democrats on the Oversight and Accountability Committee in the next Congress, putting the feisty liberal veteran in one of the most powerful spots to confront President-elect Trump in his second term.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota-backed bill to make bald eagles the national bird heads to Biden’s desk
item.description: The bald eagle could soon become the national bird of the United States after a bill backed by Minnesota legislators passed the U.S. House and Senate.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Government funding bill will run through March, include $100B in disaster aid
item.description: Pieces of a bipartisan effort to prevent a government shutdown during the holiday season are beginning to come into focus, including a March end date and $100 billion in disaster aid. GOP leadership briefed Republicans on Tuesday morning on some of the details of the forthcoming legislation.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Americans end 2024 with grim economic outlook, but Republicans are optimistic for 2025: AP-NORC poll
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The unemployment rate is healthy and the stock market is up, but Democrats are feeling more pessimistic about the U.S. economy after Donald Trump's election victory, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida GOP lawmaker files bill banning political flags in public buildings
item.description: If one state senator can shore up enough support in Tallahassee, political flags will no longer be allowed to be flown in public buildings.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge rules Trump's conviction withstands Supreme Court immunity decision
item.description: A New York judge upheld a jury’s verdict that convicted President-elect Trump of a felony, ruling the outcome of the hush money case can withstand the Supreme Court’s new test for presidential immunity.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Journalists anticipate a renewed hostility toward their work under the incoming Trump administration
item.description: For the press heading into a second Trump administration, there's a balancing act between being prepared and being fearful.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden establishes a national monument for Frances Perkins, the 1st female Cabinet secretary
item.description: President Joe Biden on Monday established a national monument honoring the late FDR-era Labor Secretary Frances Perkins, the first woman to serve in a presidential Cabinet and a driving force behind Franklin D. Roosevelt's New Deal programs after the Great Depression.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, AAMER MADHANI and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Paris Hilton heading to Capitol with 'urgent' plea to pass youth welfare program reform
item.description: Paris Hilton is heading back to the Capitol with an urgent plea, calling on "every member of the House" to pass a bill focused on youth congregate care facilities that she says would "create real, meaningful change" before the legislative session ends this week.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: Trump girds for battle with Democrats, Supreme Court over birthright citizenship
item.description: President-elect Trump, Democratic lawmakers and the Supreme Court are on a collision course over restricting birthright citizenship, which Trump has identified as a top priority for his incoming administration.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Former FBI informant will plead guilty to lying about phony bribery scheme involving the Bidens
item.description: A former FBI informant is set to plead guilty to lying about a phony bribery scheme involving President Joe Biden and his son, Hunter, that became central to a Republican impeachment inquiry in Congress.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Lawmakers struggle to reach deal to avert government shutdown
item.description: Congressional negotiators are struggling to reach a deal to keep the government's lights on past Dec. 20. Text of a continuing resolution (CR) for a government funding package was expected Sunday ahead of the looming shutdown deadline.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: ABC agrees to give $15 million to Trump's presidential library to settle lawsuit
item.description: ABC News agreed to pay $15 million to Trump's presidential library to settle a lawsuit over George Stephanopoulos' inaccurate on-air assertion that Trump was found liable for raping E. Jean Carroll.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Mayor Adams seeks to deport migrants who commit crimes
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams told President-elect Donald Trump’s border czar he would work with him to deport migrants who commit violent crimes in New York City. Adams met with Tom Homan at Gracie Mansion to discuss Trump’s plans for mass deportations.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: NYC Council speaker concerned about deportation plans
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- New York City Council Speaker Adrienne Adams has concerns about Mayor Eric Adams' goal to deport migrants who commit violent crimes in the city. Mayor Adams met with incoming border czar Tom Homan on Thursday to discuss President-elect Donald Trump’s plans for mass deportations.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: RFK Jr.'s daughter-in-law met with Ratcliffe about CIA job: Washington Post
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s daughter-in-law took a private meeting with John Ratcliffe, President-elect Trump’s pick to run the CIA, in an apparent bid for a high-ranking position within the agency, The Washington Post reported.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, Fox 5

item.title: Gov. Tim Walz says he’ll propose anti-fraud measures, reflects on VP bid
item.description: In his first interview with MPR News since his vice presidential run, Tim Walz said he still has “privilege” of serving as Minnesota governor, urged lawmakers to work together in divided government.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Nevada Attorney General Aaron Ford exploring run for governor in 2026
item.description: Democratic Nevada Attorney General Aaron Ford is exploring a run to become his party's nominee for governor in 2026.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News
